visual_elements.description: The water cycle, also known as the hydrological cycle, is a continuous process that describes how water moves above, on, and below the surface of Earth.

This movement involves several key processes: evaporation (the conversion of water from liquid to gas due to heat), transpiration (evaporation through plants), condensation (gas turning back into liquid in clouds forming as a result), precipitation (rain or snow falling from clouds back to Earth's surface, and collection where bodies of water accumulate rainwater.
The sun plays a critical role by providing the energy needed for evaporation. Water cycle processes are influenced by geographical location, seasonal variations, as well as human activities.
